expectations.text: Includes hero section with headline, subheadline, and CTA
expectations.evidence: Has headline ('Stop Optimizing for Revenue. Start Optimizing for Profit.'), subheadline, CTA button ('See Your True Channel Profitability'), and supporting text.

expectations.text: Has two distinct content blocks below the hero
expectations.evidence: Two blocks: 'ROAS Is Lying to You' (problem section) and 'From Connected Data to Confident Decisions in Three Steps' (how it works).

expectations.text: Headline leads with benefit/outcome, not just the product name
expectations.evidence: 'Stop Optimizing for Revenue. Start Optimizing for Profit.' — clear benefit-first headline using antithesis.

expectations.text: Includes social proof elements or placeholders
expectations.evidence: Includes a proof point: 'E-commerce brands using MetricShift reallocate an average of 30% of their ad budget within the first 60 days.' Qualifying text: 'Built for marketing leaders at e-commerce companies doing $10M-$100M in annual revenue.'

expectations.text: Uses specific data or numbers rather than vague claims
expectations.evidence: 'under 10 minutes' setup, '30% of ad budget within first 60 days', '$10M-$100M in annual revenue'. Good specificity.

expectations.text: Authoritative B2B tone without corporate jargon
expectations.evidence: Professional, direct tone. Uses relevant industry terms (ROAS, COGS, contribution margin) without corporate buzzwords. Clean and confident.
